segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This led to a number of problems.
segments.zh: 这导致了一系列问题。首先，食物竞争加剧，一些大象因此挨饿。由于国家公园内的食物有限，有些大象开始到更远的地方觅食。大象经常推倒公园周围的围栏，维修费用很高。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Firstly, there was more competition for food, which meant that some elephants were suffering from hunger.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As there was a limit to the amount of food in the national park, some elephants began looking further afield.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Elephants were routinely knocking down fences around the park, which then had to be repaired at a significant cost.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: To solve this problem, the decision was made to move dozens of elephants from Majete National Park to Nkhotakota Wildlife Park, where there were no elephants.
segments.zh: 为了解决这个问题，他们决定将数十头大象从马杰特国家公园迁移到恩科塔科塔野生动物保护区，那里原本没有大象。但显然，要将大量大象迁移到300公里外的新家，是一项相当大的挑战。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But, obviously, attempting to move significant numbers of elephants to a new home 300 kilometres away is quite a challenge.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So how did this translocation process work in practice?
segments.zh: 那么，这个迁移过程在实践中是如何进行的呢？大象以8到20头为一组进行迁移，每组都属于同一个家族。由于大象非常重视家族关系，它们必须同时被迁移。一组兽医和公园护林员乘坐直升机在公园上空飞行，锁定一个象群，将它们围拢并引导到指定的开阔平原。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Elephants were moved in groups of between eight and twenty, all belonging to one family.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Because relationships are very important to elephants, they all had to be moved at the same time.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A team of vets and park rangers flew over the park in helicopters and targeted a group, which were rounded up and directed to a designated open plain.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The vets then used darts to immobilise the elephants – this was a tricky manoeuvre, as they not only had to select the right dose of tranquilliser for different-sized elephants but they had to dart the elephants as they were running around.
segments.zh: 然后，兽医使用飞镖麻醉大象——这是一个棘手的操作，因为他们不仅要为不同体型的大象选择合适的镇静剂剂量，还要在大象奔跑时进行射击。而且必须尽快完成，以尽量减少压力。一旦大象开始倒地，团队就上前照料它们。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This also had to be done as quickly as possible so as to minimise the stress caused.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As soon as the elephants began to flop onto the ground, the team moved in to take care of them.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: To avoid the risk of suffocation, the team had to make sure none of the elephants were lying on their chests because their lungs could be crushed in this position.
segments.zh: 为了避免窒息的风险，团队必须确保没有大象胸部朝下躺着，因为这种姿势会压迫肺部。所以所有大象都必须侧卧。每头大象都有一名工作人员陪同，等待兽医进行检查。密切关注它们的呼吸非常重要——如果每分钟呼吸少于六次，大象就需要紧急医疗救助。每个象群的领头象都戴上了项圈，以便在新家追踪它们的活动。测量了每头大象的象牙——象牙大的大象更容易受到偷猎者的威胁——还测量了它们的脚。然后，大象被带到恢复区，之后装上卡车运往新家。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So all the elephants had to be placed on their sides.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One person stayed with each elephant while they waited for the vets to do checks.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It was very important to keep an eye on their breathing – if there were fewer than six breaths per minute, the elephant would need urgent medical attention.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Collars were fitted to the matriarch in each group so their movements could be tracked in their new home.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Measurements were taken of each elephant's tusks – elephants with large tusks would be at greater risk from poachers – and also of their feet.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The elephants were then taken to a recovery area before being loaded onto trucks and transported to their new home.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The elephants translocated to Nkhotakota settled in very well and the project has generally been accepted to have been a huge success – and not just for the elephants.
segments.zh: 迁移到恩科塔科塔的大象适应得很好，该项目普遍被认为取得了巨大成功——而且不仅仅是对大象而言。就业前景大大改善，提高了整个社区的生活水平。偷猎不再是问题，因为以前的偷猎者能够找到更可靠的收入来源。事实上，他们中的许多人自愿交出武器，因为这些武器对他们来说已经没用了。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Employment prospects have improved enormously, contributing to rising living standards for the whole community.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Poaching is no longer an issue, as former poachers are able to find more reliable sources of income.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In fact, many of them volunteered to give up their weapons, as they were no longer of any use to them.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: More than two dozen elephants have been born at Nkhotakota since relocation.
segments.zh: 自迁移以来，恩科塔科塔已经出生了二十多头小象。该地区面积超过1800平方公里，有足够的空间让象群继续增长。它们的存在也有助于重新平衡恩科塔科塔受损的生态系统，并提供了一种可持续的保护模式，可以在其他公园复制。所有这些都极大地吸引了旅游业，旅游业对GDP的贡献是非法野生动物贸易的五倍，这主要归功于大象。人们对……的兴趣也急剧上升。

words.def: 吸引力；有吸引力的事物

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: With an area of more than 1,800 square kilometres, there's plenty of space for the elephant population to continue to grow.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Their presence is also helping to rebalance Nkhotakota's damaged ecosystem and providing a sustainable conservation model, which could be replicated in other parks.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: All this has been a big draw for tourism, which contributes five times more than the illegal wildlife trade to GDP, and this is mainly because of the elephants.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There's also been a dramatic rise in interest ...

pairs.note: 'damage' 被替换为 'knocking down'，表示破坏。
'in the park' 被替换为 'around the park'，表示公园周围。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问公园里什么被损坏，定位句提到大象撞倒公园周围的栅栏，所以答案是 fences。

items.prompt: a suitable group of elephants from the same 32 ________ was selected

pairs.q: a suitable group of elephants from the same family
pairs.note: 题干中的 'from the same family' 对应原文 'belonging to one family'，都是指同一家族。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'Elephants were moved in groups... all belonging to one family'，与题干 'a suitable group of elephants from the same family' 匹配，因此答案填 family。

items.prompt: vets and park staff made use of 33 ________ to help guide the elephants into an open plain

pairs.note: staff 替换为 rangers，指公园工作人员

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到‘A team of vets and park rangers flew over the park in helicopters’，其中‘vets and park rangers’对应题干‘vets and park staff’，‘flew over ... in helicopters’对应‘made use of helicopters’，从而定位答案‘helicopters’

items.prompt: this process had to be completed quickly to reduce 34 ________

pairs.note: 题干中的'completed quickly'被替换为'done as quickly as possible'，意思相同但表达不同。
题干中的'stress'在原文中直接出现，是原词复现。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'This also had to be done as quickly as possible so as to minimise the stress caused'，其中'done as quickly as possible'对应题干'completed quickly'，'minimise'对应'reduce'，'stress'直接出现，因此答案就是'stress'。

items.prompt: elephants had to be turned on their 35 ________ to avoid damage to their lungs

pairs.note: 'turned on their' 和 'placed on their' 都表示将大象放置在某个姿势上，是同义替换。
题干说避免肺部损伤，原文说肺部可能被压碎，是同义改写。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到所有大象必须侧躺（placed on their sides），与题干要求将大象转向侧面（turned on their sides）对应，且原文解释了原因是为了避免肺部被压碎。

items.prompt: elephants' 36 ________ had to be monitored constantly

pairs.note: monitor 和 keep an eye on 都表示'密切关注'
their 指代 elephants，breathing 直接对应

paraphrase.explain: 题干说大象的呼吸需要持续监测，原文中'keep an eye on their breathing'直接对应，'keep an eye on'替换了'monitored constantly'。

items.prompt: data including the size of their tusks and 37 ________ was taken

pairs.p: Measurements were taken of each elephant's tusks
pairs.note: 题干中'size of their tusks'对应原文'Measurements were taken of each elephant's tusks'，'size'被替换为'Measurements'，但'tusks'直接出现。
题干中'feet'直接对应原文'feet'，是原词重现。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'Measurements were taken of each elephant's tusks ... and also of their feet'，与题干'data including the size of their tusks and feet was taken'完全对应，因此答案填'feet'。

pairs.note: opportunities 和 prospects 都表示'机会'，是近义词替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的 opportunities 对应原文中的 prospects，定位到 Employment prospects 这一句。

items.prompt: a reduction in the number of poachers and 39 ________

pairs.q: reduction in the number of poachers
pairs.p: Poaching is no longer an issue
pairs.note: 题干说减少偷猎者数量，原文说偷猎不再是问题，意思一致。
题干和原文都直接出现weapons，完全对应。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到偷猎不再是问题，前偷猎者自愿交出武器，与题干‘减少偷猎者和武器’对应。

items.prompt: an increase in 40 ________ as a contributor to GDP

pairs.note: 'a big draw for' 表示吸引，与 'increase in' 对应，都表示增长或吸引更多。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'All this has been a big draw for tourism, which contributes five times more than the illegal wildlife trade to GDP'，其中 'tourism' 是答案，'a big draw for' 对应 'increase in'，'contributes ... to GDP' 对应 'contributor to GDP'。
